key_insight: A combined CEL simulation and ANN model can accurately predict weld temperature, axial force, and defect formation in HDPE friction stir welding, enabling fast process-window optimization.
engineering_relevance: Bottom-surface preheating significantly improves weld stability by reducing thermal gradients and lowering plunge-force peaks, supporting more robust thermoplastic joint design.
method: 3D coupled Eulerian-Lagrangian (CEL) model of FSW under varied rotational speed, welding speed, and preheating
Two multilayer perceptron ANNs trained on experimental data to predict temperature and axial force histories
Validation against experiments and process-map generation for defect-free vs defective welding windows
evidence_signal: The ANN models achieved strong predictive performance with R2 values between 0.91 and 0.99, and the CEL model matched experimental trends in void formation, thermal response, and force response.
watch_item: The process map is based on the tested HDPE setup and parameter range, so broader use on other tool geometries, polymers, or larger process spaces will need additional validation.
paper_title: Computational and machine learning modelling approaches for weld quality predictions in friction stir welding of high-density polyethylene
